summary: Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting focused on tornado recovery updates and a public hearing for zoning case EC-2022-005 (Pebble Groves development). Staff provided extensive updates on disaster relief efforts, unmet needs coordination, insurance claims, and mutual aid from 31 municipalities for debris cleanup. The zoning hearing addressed Grand Communities' proposal for a 113-acre, 310-home planned development residential (PDR) community on Linton Road, with public testimony and board deliberation on conditions including lot sizes, setbacks, traffic studies, and architectural standards.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed 3-0
Motion to approve payment of bills (June 29 through August 2, 2022) passed 3-0
action_items: Steve Pegram to continue pursuing the 25 uninsured destroyed properties threshold to unlock state funding programs
Harry Holbert to find a third-party engineer/inspector for manufactured home inspections, funded through EMA
Harry Holbert to continue working with state and county prosecutor on Combs and Clock property cleanup
Chad Meadows to coordinate with EMA on debris disposal after August 5 mutual aid conclusion
Cheryl Hall to continue pursuing insurance claims for township property damage
Sam Houserman, Harry, and Steve to bring proposals to the board on allowing RVs during new house construction and accessory structure rule changes
Traffic engineer to potentially study Linton Road to Manila Road corridor per Trustee Allen's concern
Grand Communities to provide streetlight plan, dwelling unit calculations, HOA documents, and sidewalk layout at final development plan (FTP) review
Aaron Ellerman (chaplain) to chair the Long-Term Recovery Committee for tornado relief
key_amounts: $12,500 donation from Mercy Health Clermont County through Connect Clermont
$10,000 Duke Energy grant awarded for fire department
$855,000 uninsured loss threshold required for state public assistance eligibility
Several million dollars in damage to township-owned property
$200,000 to $500,000 price range for proposed Pebble Groves homes
4,200 loads of debris hauled from tornado cleanup
44 families registered with EMA hotline for unmet needs
23 of 25 required uninsured destroyed properties identified so far
113 acres and 310 homes proposed in Pebble Groves development
notable_topics: Tornado recovery and disaster relief coordination including Long-Term Recovery Committee transition
State and federal disaster funding thresholds and application processes
Mutual aid from 31 municipalities for debris cleanup
Insurance claims for township building and vehicle damage
Zoning case EC-2022-005: Pebble Groves PDR development on Linton Road (113 acres, 310 homes)
Traffic impact study for proposed development
Connectivity between proposed development, school district, and county park
Fire Station 18 structural assessment with forensic engineer
Motorola tower and communications infrastructure damage
Recycling dumpster relocation after tornado damage
Local government fund passage by Clermont County Commissioners
attendees: Harry Holbert (Terry Holbert - Planning and Zoning Director)
notes: Meeting was heavily focused on tornado aftermath recovery operations. The zoning hearing for Pebble Groves (zoning case EC-2022-005) was extensive with detailed staff presentation. The transcript was truncated before the board completed deliberation and voting on the zoning case. Deputy Yates funeral from Clark County Sheriff's Office was mentioned. Fire Chief Myers piped at the funeral ceremony. West Chester fire department sent thanks for mutual aid coverage. Goshen Township Zoning Commission recommended approval 4-0. Clermont County Planning Commission also recommended approval. The development would be Goshen Township's first roundabout. 31 municipalities provided mutual aid for debris cleanup with 8 present on meeting day.
title: Spc. BOT Mtg 8.2.22.mp4
